tiles.title: Avg Age by fNAME
tiles.insight: Perry leads with the highest average age at 71.00, while Morten has the lowest at 46.00. This 25-year age gap between the top and bottom suggests that there may be significant differences in the student populations.

tiles.title: Age distribution
tiles.insight:
Most students are around 21 years old, making up 36% of the total. However, the majority of students are actually in their 40s, with 19 students in that age group. 

So what: This suggests that our student body may be skewed towards older students, which could impact course planning and resource allocation.

tiles.title: fNAME breakdown
tiles.insight: Most students have a first name that falls into one of three groups: Bjørn, Emma, and Odd, making up 43% of the records. The remaining 57% of students have one of the other five first names, showing a relatively balanced distribution of names in the dataset.

tiles.title: Correlation with Age
tiles.insight: The more years of age, the more study hours students tend to have, but this relationship is quite weak. This suggests that other factors, like entry exams and programming skills, have a more significant impact on students' progress than their age.

tiles.title: Missing values per column
tiles.insight:
The majority of missing values in our education data are found in the Python column, with only 2 entries missing. Fortunately, these missing values were easily filled in during cleaning, so our analysis is unaffected. 

So what, we can proceed with our analysis without worrying about missing data in this area.
